refnamediv.refpurpose: Tests if a geometry is well-formed in 2D.

refsection.title: Description
refsection.para: Tests if an ST_Geometry value is well-formed and valid in 2D according to the OGC rules. For geometries with 3 and 4 dimensions, the validity is still only tested in 2 dimensions. For geometries that are invalid, a PostgreSQL NOTICE is emitted providing details of why it is not valid.
For the version with the flags parameter, supported values are documented in This version does not print a NOTICE explaining invalidity.
For more information on the definition of geometry validity, refer to

note.para: SQL-MM defines the result of ST_IsValid(NULL) to be 0, while PostGIS returns NULL.

refsection.para: The version accepting flags is available starting with 2.0.0.

note.para: Neither OGC-SFS nor SQL-MM specifications include a flag argument for ST_IsValid. The flag is a PostGIS extension.

refsection.title: Examples
refsection.programlisting: SELECT ST_IsValid(ST_GeomFromText('LINESTRING(0 0, 1 1)')) As good_line, ST_IsValid(ST_GeomFromText('POLYGON((0 0, 1 1, 1 2, 1 1, 0 0))')) As bad_poly --results NOTICE: Self-intersection at or near point 0 0 good_line | bad_poly -----------+---------- t | f
